atoms.content: Machine learning models can only understand numbers, not text. Encoding is the process of converting categorical text data (like 'Red', 'Blue') into numerical formats.
Label Encoding assigns a unique integer to each category (e.g., Red=1, Blue=2). This can trick models into thinking Red is 'less than' Blue.
One-Hot Encoding creates a new binary column for every category. A row will have a 1 in the 'Is_Red' column and 0s elsewhere.
Label encoding is grading sizes (Small=1, Medium=2). One-hot encoding is a checklist where you can only check exactly one box.
If you have a column of colors with no logical ordering, which encoding method should you use? (Answer: One-Hot Encoding).
Ordinal variables have a natural order (Small < Medium < Large). Nominal variables have no order (Red, Blue, Green). The encoding method depends on which type you have.

1. Label Encoding for ordinal data.
2. One-Hot Encoding for nominal data.
3. Drop one column to avoid the dummy variable trap.
4. Target Encoding uses the mean of the target per category.
